response.justification: Candidate brings 15+ years of polyglot full-stack experience with relevant exposure to high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic millions of req/sec), current AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, and a Staff title. However, the resume shows no demonstrable Python expertise — a must-have — and distributed systems depth appears limited to one role years ago. Strengths in craftsmanship, ownership, and current AI tooling work warrant a screening conversation to verify Python proficiency and distributed systems depth.
strengths: Current hands-on work with MCP and agentic workflow tooling at re:cinq aligns with the AI-native mindset requirement
Prior high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/sec, low-latency async)
Strong software craftsmanship signal: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus teaching/workshop track record
concerns: No evidence of Python in skills list or job history — a must-have for the role
Distributed systems depth is concentrated in a single 2018-2020 role; recent work appears more product/full-stack oriented
Limited signal on stakeholder management at scale, technical strategy leadership, or formal on-call/incident response ownership
